category.name: Hyphenation
category.preferred_workflow: Use native Aspose.Words hyphenation APIs directly with local deterministic sample inputs and local dictionary files where needed.

strict_api_bans: Do not assume source documents, folders, or hyphenation dictionary files already exist.
Do not invent unsupported hyphenation APIs or callback hooks.
Do not depend on external dictionary downloads for live examples.
Do not skip writing the requested output artifact when the task expects one.
canonical_patterns: Use documented Aspose.Words hyphenation APIs directly.
Create local sample documents and local deterministic dictionary files whenever a task implies an existing source or dictionary.
Use narrow layout or other deterministic formatting only when the task requires visible hyphenation behavior.
Validate that the configured result, report, or rendered output actually exists.
nullable_warning_prevention: Initialize all non-nullable reference type properties to avoid CS8618 warnings.
Avoid CS8600, CS8602, and CS8604 by guarding maybe-null values before dereference or assignment.
Declare nullable locals when a value may legitimately be null and null-check before use.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Example depends on a GitHub or other external URL for a hyphenation dictionary file.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Create a deterministic local dictionary file inside the example and register that local file instead.

common_failure_patterns.pattern: Wrong or invented hyphenation API usage
common_failure_patterns.symptom: Compile failures or ineffective behavior because unsupported hyphenation helpers or callback hooks are used.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Use only documented Aspose.Words hyphenation registration and callback APIs.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Example configures hyphenation but the layout never becomes narrow enough for hyphenation to matter.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Use deterministic page or layout settings and sufficiently long sample text so hyphenation can realistically occur.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Example assumes an existing document, stream, folder, or dictionary already exists.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Create local sample documents, source files, folders, and dictionary files inside the example.

examples.task: Load a DOCX file, register a custom Hunspell dictionary, and enable automatic hyphenation.
Register an external LibreOffice dictionary from GitHub and apply it to documents written in Spanish.
Create a script that downloads the latest LibreOffice hyphenation dictionaries and registers them automatically.
Update an existing Hunspell dictionary by adding custom hyphenation patterns for technical terminology.
Set the hyphenation language to en‑US and verify word breaking matches Microsoft Word behavior.
Load a DOCX containing mixed languages, set appropriate hyphenation language for each section.
Set hyphenation to respect word boundaries in Arabic script by loading appropriate right‑to‑left dictionary.
Configure hyphenation to respect compound‑word rules of Microsoft Word for German language documents.
Set minimum word length for hyphenation to five characters and observe its effect on short words.
Validate that hyphenation does not occur for words shorter than the configured minimum length.
Enable hyphenation globally for a document, then override it for a single table cell.
Disable hyphenation for headings while keeping it enabled for body paragraphs in a report.
Apply hyphenation only to a selected range using DocumentBuilder and verify layout changes.
Apply hyphenation to a specific paragraph by disabling it for the surrounding sections.
Load a PDF file, enable hyphenation, and render the result to an image for visual inspection.
Use the API to query hyphenation status of each word in a paragraph and log the results.
Check whether the word 'hyphenation' will be hyphenated using the API before document generation.
Develop a function that returns true if a given word will be hyphenated under current settings.
Retrieve the hyphenation patterns for French language and log them for debugging purposes.
List all available hyphenation dictionaries on the system and display their language codes.
Programmatically adjust paragraph justification after hyphenation to prevent excessive white space gaps.
Apply hyphenation to a document, then programmatically adjust line spacing to improve readability.
Measure pagination differences after enabling hyphenation on a multi‑section report document for analysis.
Compare layout differences between a document saved with hyphenation disabled and the same document with hyphenation enabled.
Load multiple documents from a folder, enable hyphenation, and save each as a hyphenated PDF.
Batch process a collection of DOCX files, applying language‑specific hyphenation and exporting each to PDF.
Batch convert DOCX files to PDF while preserving hyphenation, and log any documents that fail.
Create a new document, enable automatic hyphenation from the start, and save it as a DOCX file.
Load a DOCX, enable hyphenation, and export the document to DOCX preserving hyphenation marks.
Enable hyphenation for a document, then export to DOCX and verify hyphenation marks are retained.
Export a hyphenated document to PDF and compare file size with a non‑hyphenated version.
Generate a PDF from a hyphenated document and ensure that hyphenation marks are not visible in the output.
Load a DOCX, set hyphenation language to ru‑RU, and save the result as PDF.
Measure rendering time differences between PDF generation with and without hyphenation enabled.
Create a console application that accepts a document path, hyphenation language code, and outputs a hyphenated PDF.
Implement error handling for loading incompatible hyphenation dictionaries and provide descriptive messages.
Integrate hyphenation dictionary updates into a CI pipeline to keep language patterns current.
Test hyphenation impact on page count by generating a long report with and without hyphenation.
Load a DOCX, disable hyphenation for footnotes only, and compare footnote layout before and after.
Create a unit test that verifies hyphenation patterns are applied correctly for Italian language documents.
